meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Freelance Pitching to Overcome Debugging Stalls
content.seo_description: Learn how to pivot from debugging frustration to profitable freelance pitching. Master Antigravity and Claude Code to build your portfolio and career.
content.answer_block: When you hit a debugging wall, stop spinning your wheels. Use Claude Code to automate the fix, then leverage that solved module as a case study for freelance pitching. By shifting from 'fixer' to 'solution architect,' you build a portfolio that trades technical frustration for high-paying client contracts.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a cryptic stack trace that mocks your progress. You have been stuck for four hours, the deadline is looming, and the imposter syndrome is settling in like a cold fog. Every developer knows the silent scream of a bug that refuses to die, but the most successful engineers know that technical debt is actually a hidden business opportunity.

Instead of drowning in logs, you are going to pivot. By using AI-driven debugging to resolve your blockers, you transform your 'can't debug' moments into verified, production-ready features. This isn't just about fixing code; it's about building a portfolio of solved problems that you can pitch to clients as a freelance solution architect.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Don't try to debug the whole file. Use precision to extract the failing logic into a standalone module.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this code block and identify the specific logic error in the state transition. Provide a refactored version that handles edge cases.

steps.title: Automate the Resolution
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to apply the fix while documenting the 'why' behind the change for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a detailed technical explanation of this bug fix, including the performance impact and scalability improvements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude repair --file ./src/services/api.ts --reason 'fix null pointer in async state'

steps.title: Package the Solution as a Service
steps.description: Turn your specific bug fix into a reusable utility or micro-service that you can offer to prospective clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Convert this logic into a modular NPM package structure that can be easily integrated into other SaaS projects.

steps.title: Draft the 'Solved Problem' Pitch
steps.description: Draft a cold pitch that focuses on the business value of the bug you just squashed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a 3-sentence outreach email to a founder explaining how this specific fix reduces server latency by 20%.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Deploy your solution to a public repo and link it to your freelance profile as proof of technical expertise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a README.md snippet that highlights the challenge, the AI-assisted approach, and the final business metric improved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit -m 'feat: add performance-optimized service module'

pro_tips: Always document the 'before' vs 'after' metrics; clients pay for outcomes, not just code.
Use Claude Code's context window to store your project's architectural patterns so your fixes remain consistent.
When pitching, focus on the 'business risk' the bug posed, not the technical syntax error.
Build a 'Fix Library' in Notion where every solved bug becomes a template for future freelance projects.
Never admit you were stuck; frame the debugging process as 'performance tuning and optimization'.
content.next_steps: Take your newly optimized module, publish it to GitHub, and reach out to three local business owners offering a free 'tech audit' based on your new skill set.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs hold your career hostage. Turn your technical roadblocks into your greatest freelance assets. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
